title: Identifying transverse and longitudinal waves
description: Practice identifying representations of transverse and longitudinal waves

assessment_items.question_content:
The dots below represent a wave traveling to the right in a uniform medium at time $t$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of wave does the image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Transverse waves are oscillations where particles are displaced perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

Longitudinal waves are oscillations where particles are displaced parallel to the direction the wave travels.
This wave travels in the same direction as the displacement of the particles from equilibrium.
The correct answer is longitudinal wave.

assessment_items.question_content:
The lines below represent a wave traveling to the right in a uniform medium at time $t$.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of wave does the image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Transverse waves are oscillations where particles are displaced perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

Longitudinal waves are oscillations where particles are displaced parallel to the direction the wave travels.
This wave travels in the same direction as the displacement of the particles from equilibrium.
The correct answer is longitudinal wave.

assessment_items.question_content:
The lines below represent a wave traveling to the bottom right in a uniform medium at time $t$.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of wave does the image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Transverse waves are oscillations where particles are displaced perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

Longitudinal waves are oscillations where particles are displaced parallel to the direction the wave travels.
This wave travels in the same direction as the displacement of the particles from equilibrium.
The correct answer is longitudinal wave.

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below represents a wave traveling to the right in a slinky at time $t$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of wave does the image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Transverse waves are oscillations where particles are displaced perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

Longitudinal waves are oscillations where particles are displaced parallel to the direction the wave travels.
This wave travels perpendicular to the displacement of the particles from their equilibrium.
The correct answer is transverse wave.

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below represents a wave traveling downward in a slinky at time $t$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of wave does the image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Transverse waves are oscillations where particles are displaced perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

Longitudinal waves are oscillations where particles are displaced parallel to the direction the wave travels.
This wave travels perpendicular to the displacement of the particles from their equilibrium.
The correct answer is transverse wave.

assessment_items.question_content:
A uniform rope is jerked periodically at one end and a wave is set up to travel along the rope at time $t$, towards the top right as shown.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of wave does the image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Transverse waves are oscillations where particles are displaced perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

Longitudinal waves are oscillations where particles are displaced parallel to the direction the wave travels.
This wave travels perpendicular to the displacement of the particles from their equilibrium.
The correct answer is transverse wave.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student wants to create a transverse wave with a horizontal slinky.  

[[☃ image 1]]



**Which direction should the student move their hand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For a transverse wave, the displacement of particles is perpendicular to the wave's direction of travel.  The wave will travel horizontally, so the student must displace the slinky vertically, up and down.
The correct answer is up and down.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student wants to create a transverse wave with a vertical slinky.  

[[☃ image 1]]



**Which direction should the student move their hand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For a transverse wave, the displacement of the particles is perpendicular to the wave's direction of travel.  The wave will travel vertically, so the student must displace the slinky horizontally, right and left.
The correct answer is right and left.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rhythmic gymnast wants to create a transverse wave with a horizontal ribbon.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which direction should the gymnast move his hand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For a transverse wave, the displacement of the particles is perpendicular to the wave's direction of travel.  The wave will travel horizontally, so the gymnast must displace the ribbon vertically, up and down.
The correct answer is up and down.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student wants to create a longitudinal wave with a horizontal slinky.  

[[☃ image 1]]



**Which direction should the student move their hand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For a longitudinal wave, the displacement of the particles is parallel to the wave's direction of travel.  The wave will travel horizontally, so the student must displace the slinky horizontally, right and left.
The correct answer is right and left.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student wants to create a longitudinal wave with a vertical slinky.  

[[☃ image 1]]



**Which direction should the student move their hand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For a longitudinal wave, the displacement of the particles is parallel to the wave's direction of travel.  The wave will travel vertically, so the student must displace the slinky vertically, up and down.
The correct answer is up and down.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student wants to create a longitudinal wave with a slinky lying on a wedge at an angle with the horizontal, with one end fixed to the wall.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which direction should the student move their hand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For a longitudinal wave, the displacement of the particles is parallel to the wave's direction of travel.  The wave will travel along the wedge surface, so the student must displace the slinky forwards and backwards along the wedge.
The correct answer is: forwards and backwards, parallel to the wedge surface.

choices.content: Forwards and backwards, parallel to the wedge surface.
Up and down, perpendicular to the wedge surface.
